mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month nominal growth in retail sales serves as an always-on coincident indicator of aggregate consumer demand. Persistent expansion in RSAFS directly lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS growth is followed by negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of rolling 12-month periods over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates the cost of capital enough to compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and falling industrial production identify a coordinated labor-market and supply contraction that is more bearish than either signal in isolation. Their joint occurrence predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month and INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 2-year rolling window, or a linear model without the interaction term achieves equal predictive R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, monetary tightening via a rising federal funds rate signals central-bank credibility and prevents uncontrolled overheating, reducing equity risk premia and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 1.5% and FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: During triggered high-inflation tightening periods, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls signals weakening labor income and impending consumption compression, triggering immediate risk-off repositioning that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month PAYEMS prints are followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates easing supply constraints and rising operating leverage across the manufacturing sector, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings surprises materialize.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration above 2% over 3 months is followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: New residential construction leads broader construction activity and durable goods demand. A sustained uptrend in housing starts predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days via forward earnings revisions in cyclical sectors.
failure_condition: HOUST uptrends do not co-occur with positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth confirms the economy is operating above potential, supporting corporate top-line expansion and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.
